channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Saccawu accuses Pick n Pay of bad faith in section 189A process threatening 22 000 jobs
item.description: The South African Commercial Catering and Allied Workers Union (Saccawu) has strongly criticised Pick n Pay following the retailer’s decision to initiate a section 189A retrenchment process. According...

item.title: Wedge Pillow Can Help Improve Sleep, Sinus Relief, and Recovery
item.description: Sleep issues such as sleep apnea, snoring, acid reflux, and post-surgery discomfort can severely affect rest. Medical experts recommend elevating the head during sleep to improve airflow and reduce di...

item.title: BABYMONSTER Focuses on Music and World Tour Over Brand Collaborations
item.description: BABYMONSTER, a rising K-pop girl group under YG Entertainment, has chosen to temporarily decline multiple offers from global luxury brands. This decision aligns with the philosophy of YG Entertainment...

item.title: Australian Gothic aesthetics and dark romantic fashion define emerging designers at Australian Fashion Week
item.description: A new wave of emerging Australian designers showcased a distinctly dark and gothic-inspired direction at Australian Fashion Week, signalling a shift away from the country’s traditional emphasis on bri...

item.title: Family of JOOUST Student Investigates Alleged Boyfriend Following Her Death
item.description: The family of Alice Riang'a, a second-year student at Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology (JOOUST) who was tragically killed, is seeking to identify her alleged boyfriend. Foll...

item.title: Six killed and several injured after temple wall collapse in Sangli due to heavy rains
item.description: At least six people lost their lives and around fourteen others were injured after a temple wall collapsed in Sangli district of Maharashtra on Tuesday. According to reports cited by PTI, the tragic i...

item.title: Strategies for Alberta to Increase Influence Within Canada
item.description: The article by Carson Jerema discusses ways Alberta can assert more influence within Canada without pursuing full independence. Jerema critiques the separatist narrative that Alberta is powerless unde...

item.title: Physical Editions and Vinyl Soundtrack Released for ‘Wanderstop’
item.description: The beloved cosy game Wanderstop, which launched digitally in March 2025, is expanding its offerings with physical editions and a vinyl soundtrack. Developed by Annapurna Interactive, the game allows ...

item.title: Jigawa Governor mourns late former lawmaker Abba Anas Adamu who died in bandit abduction
item.description: Governor Umar Namadi of Jigawa State don express deep sadness over the death of former House of Representatives member, Abba Anas Adamu. The governor describe the passing of the politician as big loss...

item.title: Government Allocates 2.6 Billion Shillings to Upgrade Oldest Hospitals Including Bombo
item.description: The Tanzanian government is taking important steps to improve health services by renovating the country's oldest hospitals. During a parliamentary session in Dodoma, Deputy Minister for Health Dr Flor...

item.title: Climate Change to Alter Distribution of Crop Pests and Diseases in Africa's Great Lakes Region
item.description: A new study by plant pathologists has shown that rising temperatures in Africa's Great Lakes region will change how crop diseases and pests spread. The research focused on key food security crops like...

item.title: WNBA tunnel walks gain prominence as players showcase diverse fashion styles
item.description: The WNBA tunnel walk has emerged as a significant fashion platform, drawing attention comparable to major fashion events. While the NBA popularized the pregame tunnel arrivals where players display th...

item.title: Efforts to include more African genetic diversity in global genomic research
item.description: For a long time, most genomic studies around the world have used DNA data mainly from people of European background. This has created big gaps in our understanding because modern humans started in Afr...

item.title: Hawks foil attempt by Gauteng officers to steal cocaine during major 2021 Aeroton drug bust
item.description: In testimony before the Madlanga commission on Tuesday, Warrant Officer Marumo Magane detailed events surrounding a significant drug bust in Aeroton, Johannesburg in July 2021. The Hawks intercepted n...

item.title: Northern B.C. wildfire grows as crews deploy helicopters to contain flames
item.description: A new wildfire in northern British Columbia is currently burning out of control, prompting emergency crews to deploy helicopters in an effort to contain it. The fire poses a significant threat to the ...

item.title: US inflation rises sharply amid soaring energy prices from ongoing Iran conflict
item.description: US consumer prices have increased markedly due to the impact of the Iran war on global energy markets, with rising fuel costs flowing through to broader inflation figures. According to the latest data...

item.title: Iran Saw Largest Increase in Jailed Writers Globally in 2025, Per PEN America Report
item.description: According to PEN America's 2025 Freedom to Write Index, Iran recorded the sharpest rise in imprisoned writers worldwide last year, jailing 43 authors—more than any other nation except China, which led...

item.title: Vice President Emphasises Human Role in Healthcare Amid AI Advances
item.description: During the 51st convocation of AIIMS, Vice President C. P. Radhakrishnan emphasised that no technology, including AI, can substitute the ethical and compassionate presence of a doctor at a patient’s b...

item.title: Senate Colleagues Question NBI's Enforcement of ICC Warrant Against Dela Rosa
item.description: A day after the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) attempted to serve an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant against Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, his allies in the Senate raised ...

item.title: Iran presents 10 conditions to FIFA ahead of 2026 World Cup over Pride Match concerns
item.description: The Football Federation of the Islamic Republic of Iran (FFIRI) don submit ten conditions to FIFA ahead of the 2026 World Cup. The main wahala dey center around the so-called 'Pride Match' against Egy...
